abstract: An interface for the app to control its extension.
overview:
## Overview

Use this class to initially enable your contact provider app extension, making it visible to the system and other apps. To use the default domain, create a manager and call [doc://com.apple.ContactProvider/documentation/ContactProvider/ContactProviderManager/enable()]. This is an `async` call, because it may prompt the person using your app to approve turning on the contact provider.

### Signaling the app extension

You also use `ContactProviderManager` when you need to invoke the app extension on demand. For example, when your app knows new contacts are available from your server, call [doc://com.apple.ContactProvider/documentation/ContactProvider/ContactProviderManager/signalEnumerator(for:)] so the extension can fetch and provide the changed contacts.

items.description: Requests that the extension enumerate its contacts for the domain.
items.name: signalEnumerator(for:)

sections.title: Managing the contact provider manager life cycle

items.name: domain

items.description: A Boolean value that indicates whether the person using the app enabled the extension domain.
items.name: isEnabled
